articles.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
articles.raw_summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’

articles.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
articles.raw_summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

articles.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
articles.raw_summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

articles.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
articles.raw_summary: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

articles.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
articles.raw_summary: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

articles.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
articles.raw_summary:
Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system.

articles.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
articles.raw_summary:
Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them.

The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection, and it is separate
articles.summary:

articles.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
articles.raw_summary:
Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans.

The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) software kit called Outsider, per the tech giant.

articles.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
articles.raw_summary:
Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself.

articles.title: SVB Energy's Vakhshouri on the Outlook for Oil Prices
articles.raw_summary:
Sara Vakhshouri, founder of SVB Energy International, says the oil market has adapted to the Strait of Hormuz disruption and prices could fall to pre-war levels if the waterway "truly" reopens.

She speaks with David Ingles and Yvonne Man on Bloomberg's The China Show. (Source: Bloomberg)
